description_long: This sculpture depicts a reclining human figure, likely female, with a classical Greco-Roman aesthetic. The figure is partially nude, with detailed musculature and anatomical features visible along the torso and legs. The head is adorned with what appears to be a stylized headdress or hair, and the right arm is bent at the elbow, while the left arm is missing. The figure is supported by a rectangular base, which appears to be part of the same material as the body. The surface shows signs of weathering and patina, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The overall form is elongated, with the body stretched horizontally and the legs extended gracefully.
description: A weathered, reclining classical human figure sculpture with detailed anatomy and a stylized head, supported by a rectangular base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full reclining figure, with clear details of the torso, legs, and head, as well as the supporting base.
description_view_1: The left side view emphasizes the depth of the sculpture and the protruding limbs, with the head and torso profile visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the back of the figure and the base, with less anatomical detail and a more block-like appearance.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the curvature of the body and the missing left arm, with the head and legs in profile.
attribute_location_context: No visible context or environment is shown around the sculpture in the images.
